program_outcomes: Matematik, fen bilimleri ve ilgili mühendislik disiplinine özgü konularda yeterli bilgi birikimi; bu alanlardaki kuramsal ve uygulamalı bilgileri, karmaşık mühendislik problemlerinde kullanabilme becerisi.
Karmaşık mühendislik problemlerini saptama, tanımlama, formüle etme ve çözme becerisi; bu amaçla uygun analiz ve modelleme yöntemlerini seçme ve uygulama becerisi.

Karmaşık bir sistemi, süreci, cihazı veya ürünü gerçekçi kısıtlar ve koşullar altında, belirli gereksinimleri karşılayacak şekilde tasarlama becerisi; bu amaçla modern tasarım yöntemlerini uygulama becerisi.

Mühendislik uygulamalarında karşılaşılan karmaşık problemlerin analizi ve çözümü için gerekli olan modern teknik ve araçları geliştirme, seçme ve kullanma becerisi; bilişim teknolojilerini etkin bir şekilde kullanma becerisi.
Karmaşık mühendislik problemlerinin veya disipline özgü araştırma konularının incelenmesi için deney tasarlama, deney yapma, veri toplama, sonuçları analiz etme ve yorumlama becerisi.
Disiplin içi ve çok disiplinli takımlarda etkin biçimde çalışabilme becerisi; bireysel çalışma becerisi.

Türkçe sözlü ve yazılı etkin iletişim kurma becerisi; en az bir yabancı dil bilgisi; etkin rapor yazma ve yazılı raporları anlama, tasarım ve üretim raporları hazırlayabilme, etkin sunum yapabilme, açık ve anlaşılır talimat verme ve alma becerisi.
Yaşam boyu öğrenmenin gerekliliği bilinci; bilgiye erişebilme, bilim ve teknolojideki gelişmeleri izleme ve kendini sürekli yenileme becerisi.
Etik ilkelerine uygun davranma, mesleki ve etik sorumluluk bilinci; mühendislik uygulamalarında kullanılan standartlar hakkında bilgi.
Proje yönetimi, risk yönetimi ve değişiklik yönetimi gibi, iş hayatındaki uygulamalar hakkında bilgi; girişimcilik, yenilikçilik hakkında farkındalık; sürdürülebilir kalkınma hakkında bilgi.

Mühendislik uygulamalarının evrensel ve toplumsal boyutlarda sağlık, çevre ve güvenlik üzerindeki etkileri ve çağın mühendislik alanına yansıyan sorunları hakkında bilgi; mühendislik çözümlerinin hukuksal sonuçları konusunda farkındalık.

courses.name: Physics I
courses.description: Within the scope of the course, motion in one, two and three dimensions, Newton's laws, work and kinetic energy concepts, potential energy and energy conservation law, momentum and collisions, dynamics of rotation, periodic motion and introduction to wave physics will be covered.
learning_outcomes: Understands the basic concepts of mechanics.
Gains analytical thinking habits.
Problem determination, acquires the ability to identify and resolve problems.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Motion along a straight line
Hafta 4: Motion in two and three dimensions
Hafta 8: Potential Energy and Conservation of Energy
Hafta 9: Center of Mass and Linear Momentum
resources: Fundamentals of Physics, 9th Edition, D. Halliday, R. Resnick, J. Walker
Physics for Scientists and Engineers;R.A.Serway; International Ed. 2002 Third Ed.
Scientists and Engineers with Modern Physics 4/E, Douglas C. Giancoli.

courses.name: Turkish I
courses.description: The course will cover the following: teaching students the structure and usage features of the mother tongue; having students acquire the ability to use Turkish appropriately as a written or oral communication tool; developing students’ creative thinking, criticizing, researching, questioning and constructive structure of the outstanding works of art in Turkish Literature; providing the language as complete and cooperated in education and making students aware of their mother tongue during the university period.
learning_outcomes: Students will be able to comprehend the structural and functional features of their mother tongue; Students will gain the ability to make a correct and good use of Turkish as a written and spoken tool linking language and thought.; enrichening students’ vocabulary knowledge; Students will develop their critical, analytical, inquisitive and creative expression skills with the exclusive works of Turkish and World Literature.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: What is language?; The birth, development, and features of language; Linguistic Families and their classification; What are colloquial language and written language, mother tongue, dialect, and accent?; The description and importance of the connection between language-culture-civilization and language-thought-feeling.

Hafta 2: Approaches to problems in Turkish Language, The features of Turkish Language, The historical development and the periods of Turkish Language, The situation of Turkish Language in Turkey and in the world.
Hafta 3: Main spelling rules and punctuation marks of Turkish language.
Hafta 4: Structural features of Turkish words
Hafta 5: Sentence formation in Turkish and types of sentences; general, semantic, and structural features of sentences inTurkish language
Hafta 7: Comprehending the phases of Turkish language through texts from 19th century to today.
Hafta 8: The genres and features of novels, short stories, and essay as examples of fictional works?
Hafta 9: Intellectual writing genres: articles, anecdotes, essays, columns, critiques, biography, autobiography, travel writing and memorandums.
Hafta 10: Development process of novel in Turkish literature
Hafta 11: A novel analysis – The analysis of Yaşar Kemal’s Yılanı Öldürseler
Hafta 12: A short story analysis – The analysis of Haldun Taner’s Onikiye Bir Var/Sancho’nun Sabah Yürüyüşü/Gülerek Ölmek
Hafta 14: Written expression: petition, CV, official report, business letter, formal correspondence, minutes
resources: Zeynep Korkmaz ve diğer, Türk Dili ve Kompozisyon Bilgileri, Yargı Yayınevi, 2003.
Emin Özdemir, Sözlü-Yazılı Anlatım Sanatı: Kompoziyon, Remzi Kitabevi, 2005.
Haldun Taner, Onikiye Bir Var/Sancho’nun Sabah Yürüyüşü/Gülerek Ölmek.
Yaşar Kemal, Yılanı Öldürseler.
Muhittin Bilgin, Anlamdan Anlatıma Türkçemiz, Kültür Bakanlığı Yayınları, 2002.
TDK Yazım Kılavuzu, TDK Yayınları, 2008.

courses.name: Academic English I
courses.purpose: This integrated and theme-based course aims to improve students' reading and writing skills as well as their academic vocabulary in English. It also aims to develop critical thinking through challenging tasks and activities that help students comprehend, evaluate, synthesize, and apply information and ideas in academic contexts.
courses.description: The course covers various academic topics through motivating and challenging learning materials. It introduces students to the most frequently used academic language skills relevant to the academic environment, with a focus on reading and writing tasks such as understanding assumptions, identifying the writer’s position, interpreting visual data, summarizing arguments, and writing effective emails and summary response essays.
learning_outcomes: Students can identify main ideas and supporting details in linguistically complex academic texts.
Students can distinguish between facts, opinions, and arguments by analyzing an author’s purpose, attitude, and reasoning.
Students can preview texts using titles, visuals, and headings to make predictions and inferences about the content and structure.
Students can scan academic texts to locate specific information such as dates, names, causes, effects, or statistics.
Students can interpret and evaluate graphics and numerical data presented within academic texts.
Students can guess the meanings of unfamiliar words and identify reference words in context to improve comprehension.
Students can synthesize information from two or more sources to respond to a question or support an argument.
Students can write well-structured formal correspondence and academic essays using appropriate format, organization, and cohesive devices.
Students can support ideas in writing with relevant examples, explanations, sources, or statistics.
Students can summarize, paraphrase, revise, and edit their written work to improve clarity, grammar, and coherence.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Course Introduction / Reading - Understanding assumptions in questions
Hafta 2: Reading - Identifying the writer’s position / Writing - Writing an effective email
Hafta 3: Reading - Interpreting visual data / Writing - Writing an effective email
Hafta 4: Reading - Identifying stance and distancing / Writing - Writing an effective email
Hafta 5: Reading - Completing flow charts / Writing - Writing an effective email
Hafta 6: Reading - Inferring meaning of technical vocabulary / Writing Task 1
Hafta 7: Reading - Identifying the sequence of past events / Writing - Writing a Summary
Hafta 8: Reading - Summarizing an argument / Writing - Writing a summary response essay
Hafta 9: Reading - Questioning while reading / Writing - Writing a summary response essay
Hafta 10: Reading - Finding similarities and differences / Writing - Writing a summary response essay
Hafta 11: Reading - Identifying in text referencing / Writing - Writing a summary response essay
Hafta 12: Reading - Identifying cause and effect / Writing - Writing a summary response essay
Hafta 13: Reading - Identifying and inferring connections / Writing - Writing a summary response essay
Hafta 14: Overall Revision of the course content and preparing for the final exam
resources: Skillful 4 – Reading and Writing (Third Edition). Warwick L. & Rogers, L. 2024. Macmillan Education
Material Booklet.

courses.name: Calculus for Engineering I
courses.description: Real numbers and the real line, intervals, absolute value, equations and inequalities involving absolute value, graphs of quadratic equations, circles, parabolas, shifting a graph, ellipses and hyperbolas. Functions, domain and range, graphs, even and odd functions, combining functions, composite functions, piecewise defined functions. Trigonometric functions, identities, graphs of trigonometric functions. Transcendental functions. Limits and continuity. Limits at infinity and infinite limits. Differentiation, applications of derivatives, integration, applications of definite integral, techniques of integration, improper integrals.
learning_outcomes: learn basic preliminaries on functions and inequalities
learn limits and continuity
learn derivatives and its applications
learn definite integral, methods of integration and its applications
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Real numbers and the real line, intervals, absolute value, equations and inequalities involving absolute value
Hafta 2: Graphs of Quadratic Equations, circles, parabolas, shifting a graph, ellipses and hyperbolas
Hafta 3: Functions, domain and range, graphs, even and odd functions, combining functions, composite functions, piecewise defined functions
Hafta 4: Trigonometric functions, identities, graphs of trigonometric functions
Hafta 14: Techniques of Integration, Improper Integrals
resources: Thomas’ Calculus, 11 th Edition; G.B. Thomas, Jr. and M. D. Weir and J. Hass; Addison-Wesley; 2009; ISBN: 978-0-321-52679-3
Calculus: A Complete Course, 7 th Edition; Robert A.Adams and C. Essex; Pearson; 2010; ISBN: 978-0-7167-2105-5

courses.name: Introduction to Biology
courses.purpose: This course introduces students to the fundamental concepts of biological science and the relationship between biology and computing. It aims to build a foundation for understanding biological systems and their applications in bioinformatics and related fields.
courses.description: An introduction to the fundamental concepts of biological science will be covered. Topics include structures and processes from molecules to organisms, patterns of inheritance, the structure and function of DNA, gene expression and regulation, genetic engineering, DNA technology and genomics. Applying engineering to biology, i.e.; bioinformatics and biological databases, synthetic biology and nanobiotechnology will also be looked over.
learning_outcomes: Understand fundamental concepts in biology and how computers can be used in biological studies.
Critically think about, analyze and evaluate scientific data and knowledge.
Understand the relationship between biology and computer.
Learn the bioinformatics terminology, and the key methods and tools used in bioinformatics.
Have a general idea of synthetic biology and nanotechnology which are the important future fields and to understand their applications.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: An Introduction to Life on Earth: What is Life? What is Science?
Hafta 2: The Life of the Cell: Atoms, Molecules, Life and Biological Molecules
Hafta 3: Cell Structure, Cell Membrane Structure and Function
Hafta 4: The Working Cell: Cellular Respiration and Photosynthesis
Hafta 5: Genetics: Cellular Reproduction, Patterns of Inheritance
Hafta 6: The Structure and Function of DNA, Gene Expression and Regulation
Hafta 7: Biotechnology: Genetic Engineering, DNA Technology and Genomics
Hafta 8: Biotechnology: Genetic Engineering, DNA Technology and Genomics
Hafta 10: Synthetic Biology: Applying Engineering to Biology
Hafta 12: Biology and Computer: Today and Future- Presentations
Hafta 13: Biology and Computer: Today and Future- Presentations
Hafta 14: Biology and Computer: Today and Future- Presentations
resources: Eric J. Simon, Jean L. Dickey, Jane B. Reece, Kelly A. Hogan, Campbell Essential Biology with Physiology. Pearson, 2016.
Jane B. Reece, Martha R. Taylor, Eric J. Simon, Jean L. Dickey, Kelly Hogan, Campbell Biology Concepts and Connections. Pearson, 2015.
Dan Gusfield, Algorithms on Strings, Trees and Sequences: Computer Science and Computational Biology. Cambridge University Press, 1997.
David W. Mount, Bioinformatics: Sequence and Genome Analysis. Cold Spring Harbor Laboratory Press, 2004.

courses.name: Ethical Principles and Social Responsibility
courses.description: The concept of ethics, the philosophy of ethics and historical background, theory of ethics, ethical dilemmas and sample practices, the relationship between socializing, values, behaviors, social behavior rules, decision making and social responsibility, work ethics, global ethics, politics of ethics and unethical practices, concepts of social responsibility and organizational social responsibility in the light of universal values; the benefits that social responsibility presents in the betterment of the quality of life of individuals, institutions and the society, and criticisms related to this topic, ethical approaches in the solution for disagreements.
learning_outcomes: Know and evaluate the definitions of ethics and social responsibility.
Explain the development processes of ethics and social responsibility and evaluate the present situation.
Know the subjects related to ethics and social responsibility.
Comment on the relationship between ethics and social responsibility and other subjects.
Evaluate the consequences of occupational corruption and unethical behaviours in work life.
Know the principles and practices of international guides and standards.
Can evaluate the similar practices in the world.
Know and comment on the process of the concepts of ethics and social responsibility in Turkey.
Know the methods used in ethics and social responsibility research.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Ethics (Conceptual definition, its philosophy and historical background)
Hafta 3: Ethical dilemmas and example practices
Hafta 7: Social behavior rules and ethics (MID TERM)
Hafta 8: Decision making process and ethics
Hafta 10: Social responsibility -I (Framework, volunteering and links with NGOs)
Hafta 11: Social responsibility -II (Organisational social responsibility, approaches and dimensions)
Hafta 12: Benefits of social responsibility and criticisms
Hafta 13: Relationship between culture, ethics and social responsibility
resources: Course notes and PPP on ethics and social responsibility (The course is open for the access of students in webonline.)
Mahmut Arslan, İş ve Meslek Ahlakı, Siyasal Kitabevi, 2005, 9756325275
Ömer Torlak, İşletmelerde Sosyal Sorumluluk ve Etik, Anadolu Üniversitesi Yayını, 2013, 978-975-06-1293-0

courses.name: Computer Programming I
courses.purpose: This course introduces the basic concepts of software and hardware and builds foundational knowledge in computer programming. It aims to equip students with the basic skills needed to program and to learn additional programming languages independently.
courses.description: An introduction to the basic concepts of software and hardware is provided. Knowledge related to computer programming is detailed. Various interesting and classical problems are used as examples to learn the C language.
learning_outcomes: Overview of computing structures.
Basic terminology used in computer programming.
Syntax of a C program.
Data types in computer programs.
Expressions
Control and loop structures
Functions and parameter passing protocols.
Arrays
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Introduction to Computers and Binary Numbers
Hafta 3: Structure of a C program
Hafta 4: Basic I/O, Declarations, and Assignment
resources: Paul Deitel and Harvey Deitel, C How to Program, Pearson, 2023, 978-1-292-43707-1
Jeri R. Hanly and Elliot B. Koffman, Problem Solving and Program Design in C, Pearson, 2015, 978-1-292-09881-4

courses.name: Physics II
courses.description: In this course, the concepts of electric charge and the electric field, Gauss's law, electric potential, capacitor and dielectrics, current, resistance and electromotive force, direct current circuits, magnetic forces, magnetic field sources, induction, alternating current and electromagnetic waves will be explained.
learning_outcomes: Gains analytical thinking habits.
Problem determination, acquires the ability to identify and resolve problems.
Will be able to comprehend the basic principles of electromagnetic theory.
weekly_topics: Hafta 9: Magnetic Fields due to Currents
Hafta 11: Induction and Inductance,Electromagnetic Oscillations and Alternating Current
Hafta 12: Electromagnetic Oscillations and Alternating Current
resources: Fundamentals of Physics, 9th Edition, D. Halliday, R. Resnick, J. Walker
Physics for Scientists and Engineers;R.A.Serway; International Ed. 2002 Third Ed.
Scientists and Engineers with Modern Physics 4/E, Douglas C. Giancoli.

courses.name: Turkish II
courses.description: The course will cover the following: teaching students the structure and usage features of the mother tongue; having students acquire the ability to use Turkish appropriately as a written or oral communication tool; developing students’ creative thinking, criticizing, researching, questioning and constructive structure of the outstanding works of art in Turkish Literature; providing the language as complete and cooperated in education and making students aware of their mother tongue during the university period.
learning_outcomes: Students will learn to express their feelings and thoughts both orally and written abiding by the punctuation rules of Turkish Language.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Written Expression 1 (Paragraph, types of paragraph)
Hafta 2: Written Expression 2 (Planning, types of planning)
Hafta 3: Elements and methods of expression
Hafta 5: Basic methods of giving references and citations
Hafta 8: Development stages of poetry in Turkish literature-1
Hafta 9: Development stages of poetry in Turkish literature-2
Hafta 10: An analysis of Cemal Süreya’s Sevda Sözleri
Hafta 11: Connections of Epic and folk story to novel and short story
Hafta 12: Oral expression 1 (Speech error and the rules of rhetoric)
Hafta 13: Oral expression 2 (Types of oral expression)
resources: Zeynep Korkmaz ve diğer, Türk Dili ve Kompozisyon Bilgileri, Yargı Yayınevi, 2003.
Emin Özdemir, Sözlü – Yazılı Anlatım Sanatı: Kompozisyon, Remzi Kitabevi, 2005.
Cemal Süreya, Sevda Sözleri, Yapı Kredi Yayınları.
Muhittin Bilgin, Anlamdan Anlatıma Türkçemiz, Kültür Bakanlığı Yayınları, 2002.
TDK Yazım Kılavuzu, TDK Yayınları, 2008.

courses.name: Academic English II
courses.purpose: This course aims to equip freshmen students with the essential academic listening and speaking skills they need to cope better with English in their academic studies. It also aims to develop students’ ability to participate effectively in academic discussions and use English in academic and professional contexts.
courses.description: ENG 122 Academic English II is a compulsory listening and speaking course with an integrated, theme-based approach. The course covers various academic topics through motivating and challenging learning materials and focuses on developing students’ listening, speaking, note-taking, discussion, and presentation-related language skills.
learning_outcomes: Students can identify main ideas, supporting details, and speaker attitudes in academic lectures and discussions.
Students can take structured notes during a lecture using abbreviations, symbols, and sequence markers.
Students can understand and interpret lecture organization by recognizing signals for cause-effect, comparison-contrast, listing, and numerical data.
Students can use contextual clues to guess the meaning of unfamiliar words in academic listening texts.
Students can express opinions, make counter-arguments, and justify their ideas using appropriate language structures and vocabulary.
Students can initiate, maintain, and conclude academic discussions using effective turn-taking strategies.
Students can organize their speech clearly using linking words and discourse markers to present and support ideas.
Students can ask for clarification, confirm understanding, and respond tactfully to others' opinions in discussions.
Students can analyze visuals and statistical data such as charts and tables and comment on them using relevant language.
Students can paraphrase ideas and use a variety of linguistic strategies to express agreement or disagreement during a discussion.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Course Introduction/Listening: Identifying Jokes and colloquial allusions
Hafta 2: Listening: Adopting a critical stance to information in lectures / Speaking: Keeping a discussion going
Hafta 3: Listening: Following the way a discussion develops / Speaking: Dealing with issues in group work
Hafta 4: Listening: Using Cornell notes for lectures / Speaking: Dealing with issues in group work
Hafta 5: Listening: Recognizing allusions to external events / Speaking: Referring to other speakers
Hafta 6: Listening: Listening to annotate presentation slides / Speaking: Referring to other speakers
Hafta 7: Listening: identifying consensus in group speech / Speaking: Reaching a consensus and justify decisions
Hafta 8: Listening: listening for speculation and degree of certainty Speaking: Reaching a consensus and justify decisions
Hafta 9: Listening: Listening to detect and repair lapses in understanding / Speaking: Drafting Persuasive statements
Hafta 10: Listening: Using extension materials to support understanding / Speaking: Drafting Persuasive statements
Hafta 11: Listening: Concurrent note-taking and listening / Speaking: Conducting successful interviews
Hafta 12: Listening: Following abstract argumentation / Speaking: Conducting successful interviews
Hafta 14: Listening: identifying patterns in lectures / Speaking: Revision
resources: Skillful 4 – Listening and Speaking (Third Edition). Pathare, E. & Pathare, G. (2024). Macmillan Education
Material Booklet.

courses.name: Calculus for Engineering II
courses.description: Sequences, infinite series, alternating series, power series, Taylor and Maclaurin series, vectors, lines and planes in space, vector valued functions, functions of several variables, partial derivatives, directional derivatives, extreme values, Lagrange multipliers, double integrals, polar coordinates, triple integrals, cylindrical and spherical coordinates, substitution
learning_outcomes: Understand the notion of a sequence, its basic properties, and the concept of a limit of a sequence
Know and apply correctly tests for convergence of positive numerical series
Construct Taylor and Maclaurin series for a given function
Use Taylor and Maclaurin series for approximation of functions, to calculate limits and estimate the error
Recognize and construct the equations of lines and planes
Apply partial derivatives for finding equations of tangent planes, normal lines, and for extreme values
Evaluate double integrals in cartesian and polar coordinates and triple integrals in cartesian and cylindrical coordinates
Apply multiple integrals for computing areas and volumes
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Infinite series, the integral test, comparison tests, the ratio and root tests, alternating series
Hafta 3: Power series, Taylor and Maclaurin series
Hafta 4: Convergence of Taylor series, error estimates, applications of power series
Hafta 5: Three-dimensional coordinate systems, cylindrical and spherical coordinate systems
Hafta 6: Vectors, the dot product, the cross product, lines and planes in space
Hafta 7: Vector functions, functions of several variables, limits and continuity in higher dimensions
Hafta 8: Partial derivatives, the chain rule
Hafta 9: Directional derivatives, tangent planes and differentials
Hafta 10: Extreme values and saddle points
Hafta 14: Triple integrals in rectangular coordinates
resources: Thomas’ Calculus; G.B. Thomas, Jr. and M. D. Weir and J. Hass; Addison-Wesley; 2009; ISBN: 978-0-321-52679-3
Calculus: A Complete Course; Robert A.Adams and C. Essex; Pearson; 2010; ISBN: 978-0-7167-2105-5

courses.name: Computer Programming II
courses.purpose: The course aims to make an introduction to software programming using Object Oriented techniques. It builds on C programming knowledge and introduces C++ programming language. At the end of this course, the students will learn how to develop object-oriented programs for various problems using C++ programming language.
courses.description: Introduction to software programming using object-oriented techniques, building on C programming knowledge and introducing the C++ programming language. The course covers pointers, strings, structures, problem solving, basic C++ programming, object-oriented programming concepts, classes, functions, operator overloading, and inheritance.
learning_outcomes: Pointers in C
Strings in C
Structures in C
Basics of programming in C++
Object-Oriented programming, Classes
Functions, operator overloading
Inheritance
resources: Paul Deitel and Harvey Deitel	C How to Program	Pearson	2023	978-1-292-43707-1
Paul Deitel and Harvey Deitel	C++ How to Program	Pearson	2017	978-1-292-15334-6
Jeri R. Hanly and Elliot B. Koffman	Problem Solving and Program Design in C	Pearson	2015	978-1-292-09881-4

courses.name: Computational Thinking
courses.description: This course covers an overview of the software engineering field, department’s academic program, rules and regulations; software engineering models and practices, ethical, legal and contemporary issues in engineering.
learning_outcomes: Understand University rules and regulations
Grasp software engineering profession with its ethical and legal expectations
Learn Software Development Life Cycle
Get an introduction to each step of software development
resources: Ian Sommerville	Software Engineering	Pearson	2015	978-0133943030
Roger S. Pressman and Bruce Maxim	Software Engineering – A Practitioner’s Approach	McGraw Hill	2014	978-0078022128
IEEE Computer Society	Guide to the Software Engineering Body of knowledge	IEEE	2014	http://www.swebok.org

courses.name: Data Structures
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to fundamental data structures and algorithms used in modern software and game development. It also helps students understand how to store and process data efficiently and how to choose appropriate algorithms and data structures for different situations.
courses.description: Data structures form the foundation of many techniques performed in modern video games and are essential to designing and developing games. The course covers efficient data storage and processing in memory, algorithm selection and comparison, and the use of data structures to support game data processing in various situations.
learning_outcomes: Formulate and apply object-oriented programming as a modern tool to solve engineering problems.
Demonstrate an understanding and design of basic data structures (such as an array-based list, linked list, stack, queue, binary search tree) and algorithms.
Demonstrate the ability to analyze, design, apply and use data structures and algorithms to solve game and engineering problems and evaluate their solutions.

Demonstrate an understanding of analysis of algorithms. Study an algorithm or program code segment that contains iterative constructs and analyze the asymptotic time complexity of the algorithm or code segment.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Data Structures, Data Structures and Algorithms, Data Structures in Games and Simulations
Hafta 2: Arrays: The Data Structures Known as Arrays
Hafta 3: Linked Structures (Singly, Doubly, Circular), Applications of linked lists
Hafta 4: Stacks & Queues (array implementation and linked list implementation)
Hafta 5: Stack and Queue applications, Infix, postfix, prefix expressions and conversion algorithms
Hafta 7: Sorting and Searching, Sort Concepts, Binary search
Hafta 8: Non Linear Lists: Basic Tree Concepts, Terminology, Tree Traversals, Binary Trees and Their Implementations, Binary Search Trees
Hafta 9: Insert, Delete, Search and Traversal Algorithms on Binary Search Trees
Hafta 10: Priority Queue, Heap Abstract Data Type
Hafta 11: Graphs: Introduction to Graphs, Searching with Graphs, Topological Sorting, Weighted Graphs
Hafta 12: Introduction to Scene Management, Game Math, Scene Graphs
Hafta 13: Binary Space Partitioning Trees, Quad-Trees and Octrees
Hafta 14: Data Compression: Introduction to Data Compression, Introduction to Texture Compression
resources: Allen Sherrod, Data Structures and Algorithms for Game Developers (Game Development Series), Charles River Media, 2007, ISBN-10: 9781584504955
Michael T. Goodrich, Roberto Tamassia, Michael H. Goldwasser, Data Structures and Algorithms in Java 6th Edition, Wiley; 6 edition (January 28, 2014), 2014, ISBN-10: 1118771338
Timothy M. Henry, Frank M. Carrano, Data Structures and Abstractions with Java, Pearson; 4 edition, 2015, ISBN-10: 1292077182
Ron Penton, Data Structures for Game Programmers, Muska & Lipman/Premier, 2002, ISBN-10: 1931841942
Michael Dawson, Beginning C++ Through Game Programming, Cengage Learning PTR; 4 edition, 2014, ISBN-10: 1305109910

courses.name: Discrete Structures
courses.purpose: The course aims to introduce the fundamental concepts of mathematical logic, sets, and functions needed for analyzing propositions, proving theorems, and solving applied problems.
courses.description: The course covers mathematical logic, sets and set operations, proofs, functions as relations and their properties, relations and partial orderings, graphs, trees, boolean algebra, combinatorics, machines and languages, and preliminary linear algebra topics such as matrix and vector operations.
learning_outcomes: Understand elementary facts about sets, functions, relations, graphs, trees, boolean algebra, machines and languages.
Conduct relation properties, representing relations and partial ordering.
Find the number of solutions to arrangement problems which are isomorphism and connectivity.
Apply knowledge on trees and traversal analysis of algorithm.
Use boolean algebra problems.
Analyze machine and languages connect algorithmic and algebraic aspects of machine and languages.
weekly_topics: Hafta 14: Equation Solving and Preliminaries of linear algebra (matrix and vector operations)
resources: Kemeth. H. Rosen, Discrete Mathematics and Its Applications, McGraw Hill, 2011, 978 0073383095

courses.name: Software Design
courses.description: This course will provide students the fundamentals of object-oriented software development by using the Unified Modeling Language (UML). It begins with the fundamentals of UML and it covers use case diagrams, class diagrams and associations, object diagrams, behavioural diagrams, sequence diagrams, collaboration diagrams, activity diagrams, state diagrams, implementation diagrams, component diagrams, deployment diagrams.
learning_outcomes: Be able to understand and apply object-oriented design techniques
Express the specifications and design of an application using UML
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Object Oriented Modelling with UML
resources: Booch G., Maksimchuk R.A., Engel M.W., Young B.J., Conallen J. ve Houston K.A., Object-Oriented Analysis and Design with Applications, 3rd Ed. Addison-Wesley Professional, 2007 978-0201895513

courses.name: Software Project I
courses.description: The laboratory includes four or more practical assignments. The students are expected to implement complex computing problems in an efficient way. The students are expected to present a well-designed documentary report with their assignments. The utilization of compilers, package programs and recent IDEs (Integrated Development Environments) are included in this laboratory.
learning_outcomes: Student will be able to develop a complete application.
Student will be able to produce algorithmic solutions for given problems.
Student will be able to use any programming language for the application that he/she develops.
Student will be able to write reports for the applications he/she develops.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Presentation of First Assignment / Computer Programming
Hafta 5: Presentation of Second Assignment / Design
Hafta 7: Presentation of Third Assignment / Design
Hafta 9: Presentation of Fourth Assignment / Data and Game Structures
Hafta 12: Presentation of Fifth Assignment / Data and Game Structures
resources: Allen Sherrod Data Structures and Algorithms for Game Developers (Game Development Series) Charles River Media 2007 ISBN-10: 9781584504955
Michael T. Goodrich, Roberto Tamassia , Michael H. Goldwasser Data Structures and Algorithms in Java 6th Edition Wiley; 6 edition (January 28, 2014) 2014 ISBN-10: 1118771338
Booch G., Maksimchuk R.A., Engel M.W., Young B.J., Conallen J. ve Houston K.A., Object-Oriented Analysis and Design with Applications, 3rd Ed., Addison-Wesley Professional, 2007 978-0201895513
Deitel, P. Java: How to Program, 9th Edition Prentice Hall 2011 9780132575669

courses.name: Principles of Atatürk and History of Turkish Revolution I
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach students about the Turkish War of Liberation, Atatürk’s reforms and principles, and Atatürk’s thoughts. It also aims to increase students’ interest in historical texts and strengthen their ability to analyze historical events from a multidimensional perspective.
courses.description: The course covers the collapse of the Ottoman Empire and the factors that prepared the Turkish Revolution, the First World War, the Armistice of Mondros, the organization of the Turkish Independence movement, the National Pact, the occupation of Istanbul, the foundation of the Grand National Assembly of Turkey, the San Remo Conference and the Treaty of Sèvres, the battles and diplomatic relations during the War of Independence, the Great Offensive, the Armistice of Mudanya, and the Treaty of Lausanne.
learning_outcomes: Students will be able to learn the Turkish Independence War and history of Turkish Republic
Evaluate and interpret the Turkish modernization process
Comprehend the principals of Ataturk and Turkish revolution
Use the information that are given during the course in their daily life
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the course and course requirements
Hafta 2: A general assessment of factors which caused the collapse of Ottoman Empire and prepare Turkish Revolution - 1
Hafta 3: A general assessment of factors which caused the collapse of Ottoman Empire and prepare Turkish Revolution - 2
Hafta 7: National Pact and occupation of Istanbul by Allied Forces
Hafta 8: Foundation of Grand National Assembly of Turkey
Hafta 9: The San Remo Conference and the Treaty of Sèvres
Hafta 10: Fights and Period of Diplomatic Relations-1 (I. and II. Inönü Fights, London Conference)
Hafta 11: Fights and Period of Diplomatic Relations-2 (The Sakarya Battle, Ankara and Moscow Agreements)
Hafta 12: The victory of Turkish (The Great Offensive and the Battle of Dumlupınar, The Armistice of Mudanya)
Hafta 13: Treaty of Lausanne and its results
Hafta 14: A general assessment of the course.
resources: Atatürk Araştırma Merkezi. (2009). Türkiye Cumhuriyeti Tarihi-I. Atatürk Araştırma Merkezi.
Yahya Akyüz, Utkan Kocatürk, Ergün Aybars ve diğerleri. (2007). Atatürk İlkeleri ve İnkılâp Tarihi I/1. Ayraç Yayınevi.
Erik Jan Zürcher. (2009). Modernleşen Türkiye'nin Tarihi. İletişim Yayınevi.
Ahmet Mumcu. (2006). Atatürk ilkeleri ve İnkılâp tarihi. Anadolu Üniversitesi Yayınları.
İlber Ortaylı. (2007). İmparatorluğun En Uzun Yüzyılı. Alkım Yayınevi.
Bülent Tanör. (1997). Osmanlı-Türk Anayasal Gelişmeleri (1789-1980). Yapı Kredi Yayınları.
Suna Kili. (2006). Türk Devrim Tarihi. T. İş Bankası Yayınları.

courses.name: Introduction to Applied Probability and Statistics
courses.description: Basics of Statistics, Axioms of Probability, Events, Conditional Probability, Independence, Bayes’ Rule, Random Variables, Joint Random Variables, Mathematical Expectation, Discrete Random Variables: Binomial, Hypergeometric, Negative Binomial, Geometric and Poison Distribution, Continuous Random Variables: Normal, Gamma and Exponential Distribution, Sampling Distributions, Central Limit Theorem, Regression, Correlation, Model Building
learning_outcomes: Students will be able to set up probability models for a range of random phenomena, both discrete and continuous.
Students will be able to apply the notions of conditional probability,
Students recognise where the use of certain standard probability distributions would be appropriate.
Students understand correlation and build models with appropriate variables.
weekly_topics: Hafta 4: Random Variables and Probability Distributions, Mathematical Expectation
Hafta 6: Hypergeometric Distribution, Negative Binomial Distribution
Hafta 10: Normal Approximation to the Binomial Distribution
Hafta 12: Sampling Distributions : Sample Distribution of Means and The Central Limit Theorem
resources: Probability & Statistics for Engineers & Scientists, 9th Edition; R. Walpole, R. Myers, S. Myers, Keying Ye; Pearson, 2016; 978-0134468914
Devore, J. L. (2011). Probability and Statistics for Engineering and the Sciences. Brooks/Cole.

courses.name: Principles of Atatürk and History of Turkish Revolution II
courses.purpose: The course aims at explaining the Lausanne Treaty and the political, social, cultural, and economic developments in the newly independent Republic of Turkey. It also examines the principles of Atatürk, his reforms, and their impact on the Turkish transformation.
courses.description: The course covers the Lausanne Treaty, Kemalist thought, and the principles of Atatürk. It also addresses the political, judicial, social, educational, cultural, and economic reforms in the Republic of Turkey, as well as domestic and foreign developments from 1923 to the post-1980 period.
learning_outcomes: Students will be able to learn about Turkish Republic's history documented academic essays.
Analyze, evaluate and synthesize today's incidents from the historical perspective.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Adequate knowledge about the Turkish revolution, The Lausanne Peace Treaty in briefly.
Hafta 2: Learn Kemalist thought and Principles of Atatürk (republicanism, nationalism, populism)
Hafta 3: Learn Kemalist thought and Principles of Atatürk (laicism, statism, revolutionism and supplementary principles)
Hafta 4: Explain the Turkish revolutions on the political area.
Hafta 5: Explain the Turkish revolutions on the judicial area.
Hafta 6: The Turkish revolutions on the social, education and cultural area.
Hafta 7: Understand the improvements on the economic area.
Hafta 8: The domestic and foreign improvements between 1923-1950-1 (Turkish-Greek Relationship, Balkanian Pact and Sadabad Pact, Musul and foreign depts Questions)
Hafta 9: The domestic and foreign improvements between 1923-1950-2 (Montreaux Act about Dardanelles and Istanbul Straits, Hatay question).
Hafta 10: The domestic and foreign improvements between 1923-1950-3 (The multy-party system and educational improvements)
Hafta 11: The domestic and foreign improvements between 1950-1980-1 (Turkey and NATO, Cyprus Question, Beginning of the Turkish-EU Relationship)
Hafta 12: The improvements between 1950-1980-2 (The emerge of Human Rights to international area, 1961 Constitution, 24 January 1980 Decisions on economic issues)
Hafta 13: Explain the improvements after 1980 (Education and Universities, terrorism, information society)
Hafta 14: General overview of the history of Turkish Republic.
resources: Atatürk Araştırma Merkezi. (2009). Türkiye Cumhuriyeti Tarihi-II. Atatürk Araştırma Merkezi.
Akyüz, Kocatürk, Aybars ve diğerleri (2007). Atatürk İlkeleri ve İnkılap Tarihi. Ayraç Yayınevi.
Herhangi bir yazar veya yazarlara ait bir Türk İnkılap Tarihi kitabı.
Nutuk (Speech). (1999). Mustafa Kemal Atatürk. Atatürk Araştırma Merkezi.

courses.name: Basic Linear Algebra
courses.description: Systems of Linear Equations. Matrices, Matrix multiplication, Special types of matrices, Echelon form of a matrix, Solving linear systems by Gauss-Jordan Reduction, Finding the inverse of a matrix, Determinants, Properties of determinants, Cofactor expansion, Cramer’s rule. Vector spaces. Subspaces, Span and Linear Independence. Basis and dimension. Row Space, Null Space. Homogeneous systems. Orthogonalization. Linear transformations. Change of basis, Transition Matrices.
learning_outcomes: Understand how to solve the systems of linear equations.

Perform the operations of addition, scalar multiplication, and multiplication, and find the transpose and inverse of a matrix, Calculate determinants using row operations, column operations, and expansion down any column and across any row.
Prove elementary statements concerning the theory of matrices and determinants
Write the relationships between A being invertible, det A, AX = 0 having a solution, the rank of A, and the rows of A being linearly independent
Find the kernel, range, rank, and nullnel, range, rank, and nullity of a linear transformationity of a linear transformation
Find the matrix associated with a linear transformation with respect to given bases, and understand the relationship between the operations on linear transformations and their corresponding matrices
Find the change-of-basis matrix
Prove statements of an algebraic nature concerning linear transformations
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Systems of Linear Equations. Matrices. Matrix multiplication
Hafta 2: Algebraic properties of matrix operations. Special types of matrices. Echelon form of a matrix
Hafta 3: Solving linear systems by Gauss-Jordan Reduction, Finding the inverse of a matrix by row reduction.
Hafta 5: Properties of determinants. Cofactor expansion. Inverse of a Matrix (via its determinant). Other applications of determinants (Cramers rule).
Hafta 6: Vectors in the Plane and in 3-Space.
Hafta 7: Vector spaces. Subspaces. Span and linear independence
Hafta 9: Row space, Null space, Nullity, Rank of a matrix.
Hafta 12: Linear Transformations from one vector space to another.
Hafta 13: Kernel and range of a linear transformation
Hafta 14: Change of bases, Transition Matrices
resources: Elementary Linear Algebra, B. Kolman and D.R. Hill, Prentice Hall, New Jersey, 8th Edition, 2004, ISBN: 0-13-085199-X
Elementary Linear Algebra, H. ANTON, WILEY,10th Edition, 2010, ISBN: 978-0-470-45821-1
Elementary Linear Algebra, L.E.Spence, A.J.Insel, S.H.Friedberg, Prentice Hall, 2000, ISBN: 0-13-716722-9

courses.name: Algorithms
courses.description: Algorithm complexity and asymptotic notation. Sorting and Searching, Balanced Search Trees. Divide and conquer approach. Sorting in linear time(counting sort, radix sort). Dynamic hashing. Greedy algorithms. Dynamic Programming. Elementary graph algorithms, Breadth-First search, Depth-First search. Minimum spanning tree and Shortest path algorithms.
learning_outcomes: To analyze time and space complexity of algorithms by using asymptotic approaches.
To solve a class of problems by using the divide-and-conquer recursive techniques.
To explain and analyze sorting algorithms including quicksort mergesort and heapsort.
To understand the concepts in greedy algorithms and graph-based algorithms.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Time and computational complexities of algorithms, Asymptotic notations
Hafta 9: Graph Algorithms: Depth-first search, strongly connected components analysis techniques
Hafta 11: Greedy Algorithms: Minimum spanning tree
resources: T. H. Cormen, C. E. Leiserson, R. L. Rivest, and C. Stein. Introduction to Algorithms, MIT Press and McGraw-Hill, 4th Edition, 2022.
Jeffrey J. McConnell. Analysis of Algorithms: An Active Learning Approach

courses.name: Software Requirement Analysis
courses.description: The requirements engineering process includes: identification of stakeholders requirements elicitation techniques such as interviews and prototyping, requirements specification, and validation; use of models: state-oriented, function-oriented, and object-oriented; documentation for software requirements, no matter how formal they are represented; i.e., Informal, semi-formal, and formal representations; structural, informational, and behavioral requirements; non-functional requirements; use of requirements repositories to manage and track requirements through the life cycle; case studies and software projects will be explained and detailed.
learning_outcomes: Learns software requirements and their role in software development
Learns software requirements development process and its activities
Learns definition, types, and stakeholders of software requirements
Learns techniques of software requirements elicitation and analysis
Learns software requirements models
Learns the basics of requirements documentation and validation
Learns the importance and steps of software requirements management
weekly_topics: Hafta 9: Requirements Specifications: Goals and Conflict Analysis
Hafta 11: Measuring Requirements and Change management
Hafta 12: Software Requirements Prioritization: Risk Analysis
Hafta 13: SRS Documents: Requirements and Diagrammatic Notations
resources: K. Wiegers and J. Beatty Software Requirements, 3rd Ed. Microsoft Press 2013 978-0-7356-7966-5
Maciaszek, Leszek Requirements Analysis and System Design, Pearson Education 2007 978-0321440365
A. van Lamsweerde Requirements Engineering: From System Goals to UML Models to Software Specifications Wiley 2009 978-8126545896
Leffingwell, D. & Widrig, D. Managing Software Requirements: A Use Case Approach, 2nd Ed. Addison Wesley 2003 978-0321122476
Hull, E., Jackson, K. & Dick, J., Requirements Engineering, 3rd Ed. Springer 2017 978-1-84996-405-0

courses.name: Introduction to Differential Equations
courses.description: First order differential equations, exact and linear equations, second order equations, Cauchy-Euler equations, undetermined coefficients, variation of parameters, higher order equations, system of differential equations, Laplace transform, inverse Laplace transform, series solutions of differential equations.
learning_outcomes: The students will learn the existence and uniqueness of the solutions.
The students will learn the techniques of solving first order equations and the higher order equations.
The students will learn Laplace transforms and the method of solving differential equations by the Laplace Transform.
The students will learn the system of differential equations and the eigenvalue method for solving systems of differential equations.
The students will appreciate the connection between natural and engineering systems and differential equations.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Definition, Classification and Properties of Differential Equations, Initial Value Problems and Their Solutions
Hafta 2: Mathematical Models and Vector Fields, 1st Order Equations: Separable, Linear Equations
Hafta 3: 1st Order Equations: Exact Equations, Finding Integrating Factors
Hafta 4: 1st Order Equations: Special Transformations, Homogeneous Equations, Bernouilli Equations
Hafta 5: 2nd Order Equations: Homogeneous Linear Differential Equations with Constant Coefficients, the Method of Undetermined Coefficients
Hafta 6: Variation of Parameters Method, Cauchy-Euler Equations, Reduction of Order
Hafta 8: Introduction to Systems of Differential Equations, 1st Order Systems
Hafta 9: Solving Homogeneous Linear Equation Systems with Constant Coefficients
Hafta 10: Definition of Laplace Transform, Properties of Laplace and Inverse Laplace Transforms, Solving Initial Value Problems by Laplace Transform
Hafta 11: Translation Property, Use of the Method of Partial Fraction, Convolution
Hafta 12: Laplace Transform of Periodic Functions and Unit Step Function
resources: Fundamentals of Differential Equations, 8th Edition; R.K. Nagle, E.B. Saff, A.D. Snider; Pearson; 2014; ISBN: 978-1-292-02382-3.
Differential Equations, 3rd Edition; Shepley L. Ross; John Wiley and Sons; 1984; ISBN: 978-0471032946
Lectures on Differential Equations; E. Akyıldız, Y. Akyıldız, Ş.Alpay, A. Erkip and A.Yazıcı; Matematik Vakfı Yayın No:1; 2003; ISBN: 1577663020.
Differential Equations and Boundary Value Problems: Computing and Modeling; Charles Henry Edwards, David E. Penney; Pearson Prentice Hall, 2008; 0132061155, 9780132061155.

courses.name: Software Project II
courses.description: The lab includes four or more practical assignments. Students are expected to effectively implement complex computational problems. Students are expected to submit a well-designed documentary report with their assignments. Use of compilers, packages, and the latest IDEs (Integrated Development Environments) is expected in this lab.
learning_outcomes: The student will be able to develop a complete application.
The student will be able to produce algorithmic solutions to given problems.
The student will be able to use any programming language for the developed application.
The student will be able to write reports for the applications developed.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: First Assignment Presentation / Data Structures
Hafta 8: Third Assignment Presentation / Software Requirements Analysis
Hafta 13: Fifth Assignment Presentation / Software Requirements Analysis
resources: K. Wiegers and J. Beatty, Software Requirements, 3rd Ed. Microsoft Press 2013 978-0-7356-7966-5
Leszek Maciaszek, Requirements Analysis and Systems Design (3rd Edition) 3rd Edition, Pearson Education, 2007, 978-0321440365
A. van Lamsweerde Requirements Engineering: From System Goals to UML Models to Software Specifications Wiley 2009 978-8126545896

courses.name: Software Project Management
courses.description: This course describes the key aspects of a software project. It begins with the job description of a software manager and then addresses those topics germane to successful software development management, including organizing the software development team; interfacing with other engineering organizations (systems engineering, quality assurance, configuration management, and test engineering); assessing development standards; selecting the best approach and tailoring the process model; estimating software cost and schedule; planning and documenting the plan; staffing the effort; managing software cost and schedule during development; risk engineering; and continuous process improvement.
learning_outcomes: The students can discuss roles, responsibilities, and management methods of the software project manager
The student can apply different software project management artifacts
The student can use various software project management tools
The student can appraise managing contracts
The student can discuss risk management aspects of software projects
The student can discuss different human resource management aspects of software projects
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Software Project Management
Hafta 3: Program management and Project Evaluation
Hafta 4: Selection of Appropriate Project Approach
Hafta 12: Managing people and organizing teams
resources: Software Project Management (Revised 5th Edition), Bob Hughes & Mike Cotterell, McGraw-Hill Publication 2009
Mastering Software Project Management: Best Practices, Tools and Techniques, Murali K. Chemuturi and Thomas M. Cagley, Jr. Ross Publishing 2010

courses.name: Software Testing for Quality Assurance
courses.description: This course will cover general concepts in Software Quality Assurance (SQA) and where Testing is as a part of SQA. Various Test Techniques, such as Boundary Value, Equivalence Partitioning, Orthogonal testing will be examined. The course will also cover Test Planning using international standards (IEEE/ISO). Additional topics will be presented on Test Metrics, Management, and Automation.
learning_outcomes: Discuss the meaning and importance of quality in relation to software systems,
Explain various software quality assurance (SQA) fundamental concepts, definitions, objectives,
Apply different testing strategies, techniques, and tools to software,
Recognize the importance of measurement in developing quality software systems and its cost implications,
Compare software quality assurance system infrastructure components such as procedures, work instructions and configuration management,
Learn software quality management components such as quality metrics and software quality costs.
resources: Daniel Galin, Software Quality Assurance: From Theory to Implementation, Pearson, 2003, 978-0201709452
Scott Loveland, Software Testing Techniques: Finding the Defects That Matter, Charles River Media, 2014, 978-162704023
IEEE Computer Society, Guide to the Software Engineering Body of knowledge, IEEE, 2014, http://www.swebok.org

courses.name: Concurrent Programming
courses.description: In this course, participants will be given operating systems and concurrent programming fundamentals. Within the scope of the course, computer hardware, address space, files, input/output, system calls, processes and threads, memory management, file systems and storage and multiple processor systems will be given. Subsequently, thread features, multi-thread programming, mutual exclusions and critical resources will be discussed.
learning_outcomes: Understands the organization of computer systems.
Understands process concepts related to thread creation, scheduling, synchronization, and deadlocks.
Understands how operating systems manage computer memory.
Understands the basic features of modern operating systems.
Designs and implements concurrent systems.
Gains the ability to use critical resource mechanisms of concurrent systems such as locks and message passing.
Understands and extends modern multithreaded software frameworks.
weekly_topics: Hafta 7: Deadlock and Object Monitor Concepts in Java
Hafta 11: Concurrent Data Structures - Advanced Concurrency
resources: Abraham Silberschatz, Peter B. Galvin and Greg Gagne, Operating System Concepts, Wiley, 2013, 978-1-118-09375-7
Goetz, Brian, Java Concurrency in Practice, Addison-Wesley Professional, 2006, 0321349601
Gonzalez, F., Mastering Concurrency Programming with Java 9 - Second Edition, Packt Publishing, 2017, 9781785887949

courses.name: Software Project III
courses.description: The laboratory includes four or more practical assignments. The students are expected to implement complex computing problems in an efficient way. The students are expected to present a well-designed documentary report with their assignments. The utilization of compilers, package programs and recent IDEs (Integrated Development Environments) are included in this laboratory.
learning_outcomes: Student will be able to develop a complete application.
Student will be able to produce algorithmic solutions for given problem
Student will be able to use any programming language for the application that he/she develops
Student will be able to write reports for the applications he/she develops.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Presentation of First Assignment / Algorithms
Hafta 5: Presentation of Second Assignment / Game Development
Hafta 7: Presentation of Third Assignment / Concurrent Programming
Hafta 10: Presentation of Fourth Assignment / Software Testing for Quality Assurance
Hafta 13: Presentation of Fifth Assignment / Concurrent Programming

courses.name: Summer Training I
courses.description: Students are required to attend and successfully complete a minimum of 20 working days summer training. The summer internship should be carried out in accordance with the rules and regulations set by the department. In both internships, students are expected to observe, describe and report their practical experiences.
learning_outcomes: Students will have the opportunity of applying their knowledge and skills gained through the courses on real-life systems
Based on their observations and analyses, students are expected to fulfill their Summer Internship requirements.
Students will have the opportunity to apply knowledge of mathematics, science, and engineering.
Students will have the opportunity to identify, formulate, and solve engineering problems.
Students will have the opportunity to understanding of professional and ethical responsibility.
Students will have the opportunity to use the techniques, skills, and modern engineering tools necessary for engineering practice.
Students will have the opportunity to design and conduct experiments, as well as to analyze and interpret data.
Students will have the opportunity to understand the company organization, services, products and goals.
Students are expected to express their work in an appropriate report format.

courses.name: Database Modelling and Design
courses.description: Database system concepts. Data modeling with ER and EER. The relational Data Model. File organizations and index structures. Relational algebra. Structured Query Language (SQL). Database Design: functional dependencies and table normalization. Introduction to database administration. A relational DBMS will be used in the term projects.
learning_outcomes: The student will be able to work in group settings to design and implementing database projects
Student will be able to create data requirements documents
Student will be able to prepare appropriate ER and EER data models by reviewing the data requirements document.
The student will be able to design an appropriate logical model for a given conceptual model.
Student will be able to develop a user interface to connect a database and query database using SQL comments
The student will be able to design an appropriate physical data model for a given logical model.
weekly_topics: Hafta 3: Entity Relationship daigram concepts and tools
Hafta 6: ER and EER to relational mapping (logical schema)
Hafta 7: ER and EER to relational mapping (logical schema) case studies
Hafta 10: Introduction, basic SQL Statements, Creating and managing tables, SQL for Restricting & sorting data, Single row functions
Hafta 11: Displaying data from multiple tables, Aggregating data using GROUP BY clause
Hafta 13: Manipulating Data, Creating and managing views
resources: “Fundamentals of Database Systems”, 7th Edition, Ramez Elmasri & Shamkant B. Navathe, Pearson, ISBN: 978-1292097619
Database: Application Development and Design, M.V. Mannino, McGraw-Hill

courses.name: Software Architecture
courses.description: Views and Viewpoints, Modularity and Component-Based Thinking, Architectural Styles, Architecture Characteristics, Technologies that Every Software Architect must know.
learning_outcomes: Understand and discuss the role of architectural design in modern software development methodologies.
Identify and prioritize architecture-related requirements and use them to select an initial architecture for a system.
Use architectural styles and views to design a software architecture and to present, discuss, and evaluate a given architectural design.
Evaluate constraints related to domain and business change in a live architecture and incrementally modify the architecture.
weekly_topics: Hafta 4: Layered Architecture and Pipeline Architecture
Hafta 5: Microkernel Architecture and Service-Based Architecture
Hafta 6: Event-Driven Architecture and Domain-Based Architecture
Hafta 7: Orchestration-Based Architecture and Microservices Architecture
Hafta 10: Mobile Applications, Rich Client and Rich Web Applications
Hafta 11: Web Applications and Service Applications
Hafta 12: Big Data Applications, IoT Applications and Blockchain
Hafta 13: Things Every Software Architect Should Know
resources: Fundamentals of Software Architecture: An Engineering Approach, 2020, Mark Richards, Neal Ford
Software Architecture Patterns 2nd Edition, Mark Richards, O’Reilly Media, August 2022, ISBN 978-1-098-13427-3

courses.name: Software Project IV
courses.description: The laboratory includes four or more practical assignments. The students are expected to implement complex computing problems in an efficient way. The students are expected to present a well-designed documentary report with their assignments. The utilization of compilers, package programs and recent IDEs (Integrated Development Environments) are included in this laboratory.
learning_outcomes: Student will be able to develop a complete application.
Student will be able to produce algorithmic solutions for given problem
Student will be able to use any programming language for the application that he/she develops
Student will be able to write reports for the applications he/she develops.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Presentation of First Assignment / Unix Shell or Concurrence
Hafta 5: Presentation of Second Assignment / Database
Hafta 7: Presentation of Third Assignment / Software Architecture
Hafta 10: Presentation of Fourth Assignment / Database
Hafta 11: Model Driven Software Development Tools examples
Hafta 13: Presentation of Fifth Assignment / Software Architecture
resources: Gary McGraw, Software Security: Building Security, Addison-Wesley, 2016, ISBN: 0321356705
Rozanski N. and Woods E, Software Systems Architecture: Working with Stakeholders Using Viewpoints and Perspectives, Addison-Wesley, 2011, ISBN: 978-03217183

courses.name: Data Analysis
courses.purpose: The goal of this course is to give knowledge of how to use Python to extract knowledge and information from data. At the end of this course students will be competent in using Python libraries to work with and analyze offline as well as online data.
courses.description: Course will start from general python programming basics, data structures, and algorithm design with a heavy emphasis on applying data analysis and visualization techniques to solve real-world problems in different domains. Topics include data representation, manipulation and clearing, visualization, regression, classification, clustering, model development and evaluation with most up-to-date Python modules.
learning_outcomes: Uncover hidden or unexpected connections, correlations, patterns, and trends to drive better decisions.
Self-configure Python programming environment
Code, compile, debug, and run Python programs
Learn language syntax and fundamental programming concepts including variables, control statements, loops, functions, lists, and classes
Use modules and tools to collect, reshape, analysis, and visualize data
get data from files (csv, html, json, xml) and relational databases,
rudiments of data cleaning,

Develop programs for various real-world problems by applying machine learning (regression, classification, clustering), and data visualization packages (numpy, Pandas, Scikit-learn) available in Python.
Evaluate data results and make optimal decisions
weekly_topics: Hafta 3: Summarizing the Data Frame – Descriptive Statistics
Hafta 10: Classification: predict labels as two or more discrete categories
Hafta 11: Clustering: detect and identify distinct groups in the data
resources: Wes McKinney
Python for Data Analysis, 2nd Edition
O’reilly

courses.name: Graduation Project I
courses.purpose: The main objective of this course is to guide students in developing an industry-oriented project under the supervision of a faculty member. It aims to help students analyze project requirements, propose an innovative solution to a complex problem with real-life constraints, and complete the project design and reporting according to standards.
courses.description: Within the scope of this course, a team of students develops an industry-oriented project under the guidance of a faculty member. The course covers project proposal, team formation, project selection, advisor assignment, project planning, market/literature research, preparation of the SRS and SDD documents, and final report and presentation.
learning_outcomes: Develop a requirements and a design document using international standards
Learn how to implement a product taking into real-life constraints and conditions
Learn how to work in a team environment
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Project Proposal and forming teams
Hafta 2: Project Selection and assigning an advisor
resources: IEEE Software Engineering Body of Knowledge, IEEE, 2014, http://www.swebok.org

courses.name: Summer Training II
courses.description: Students are required to attend and successfully complete a minimum of 20 working days summer training. The summer internship should be carried out in accordance with the rules and regulations set by the department. In both internships, students are expected to observe, describe and report their practical experiences.
learning_outcomes: Students will have the opportunity of applying their knowledge and skills gained through the courses on real-life systems.
Based on their observations and analyses, students are expected to fulfill their Summer Internship requirements.
Students will have the opportunity to apply knowledge of mathematics, science, and engineering.
Students will have the opportunity to identify, formulate, and solve engineering problems.
Students will have the opportunity to understanding of professional and ethical responsibility.
Students will have the opportunity to use the techniques, skills, and modern engineering tools necessary for engineering practice.
Students will have the opportunity to design and conduct experiments, as well as to analyze and interpret data.
Students will have the opportunity to understand the company organization, services, products and goals.
Students are expected to express their work in an appropriate report format.

courses.name: Graduation Project II
courses.description: Within the scope of this course, the project team formed for SENG 491 is expected to develop/implement the selected project according to the design produced in the previous semester. A test plan will be written and executed to validate the conformance of the developed product to the requirements. The finished product and findings will be presented to a jury of faculty members.
learning_outcomes: Implement the code for the design prepared in the previous semester
Prepare and execute a Test Plan
Learn how to work in a team environment
weekly_topics: Hafta 6: Product and Test Plan Development
Hafta 7: Product and Test Plan Development
